mission.title: open-webui: Feat: Ability to work with multiple Ollama servers
mission.description: Feat: Ability to work with multiple Ollama servers. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
On my system I have a capable CPU with a large amount of RAM that is able to run quite large models, albeit slowly. I also have an RTX 3060 which is able to run smaller models quite quickly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feat: Ability to work with multiple Ollama servers
steps.description:
Adds support for hitting OpenAI compatible UIs.

The backend uses the ENV as a simple key:value store, so to store an array of compatible APIs, I stored them as a `;` separated list like: `localhost1;localhost2;localhost3`

The backend/frontend implementations for OpenAI Compat are mostly copy
```yaml

INFO:     127.0.0.1:42702 - "GET /api/v1/chats/tags/all HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feat: Ability to work with multiple Ollama servers" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds support for hitting OpenAI compatible UIs.

The backend uses the ENV as a simple key:value store, so to store an array of compatible APIs, I stored them as a `;` separated list like: `localhost1;localhost2;localhost3`

The backend/frontend implementations for OpenAI Compat are mostly copy pastes of the original OpenAI implemenation, with the changes:
- Compat needs model labels because we

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
